Card titles and the card list on the set detail page now link through to the card detail page.
Wide tables on Decks, Games, Locations, Watchlist, and Admin gracefully drop lower-priority columns at narrow widths instead of overflowing.
archivist_note: The token foundation laid down two releases earlier was the prep work for this one. Token names stay constant; only values change. The aesthetic ride on top of the foundation — a pattern we'd repeat months later in the Folio cluster when swapping to warm charcoal.

added: Self-service password recovery. The login page now offers 'Forgot password?'; entering your email queues a recovery message; the link expires in 30 minutes and works once.
refined: Registration, password reset, and password change all share the same minimum-strength rule (8 characters, no composition requirements per current NIST guidance).
resolved: Registration previously had no minimum password length; a 1-character password would have been accepted.
archivist_note: The first transactional-email capability for the project. Future features that depend on outbound email — collection sharing, beta announcements, account verification — build on this foundation. The DNS work to legitimately send from cartarch.com (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) is part of this release too.

refined: The set detail page loads roughly 100× faster — from 18 to 27 seconds per visit down to under 100 milliseconds.
resolved: A long-standing performance problem on /sets/{set_code}: the previous version was paginating Scryfall live three times per visit. The new version reads from a local cache kept current by a background daemon.
archivist_note: Same shape of fix as a few releases earlier. The pattern keeps recurring: any code that makes external API calls during a page request is a latent outage. The discipline is to push that work to background daemons and read from local caches on the request path.

added: Watchlist — a per-user list of cards to track for later acquisition, price comparison, or memory. Two identity modes per row: a specific printing (a particular set + collector number) or any printing of a card name.
Add cards from the card-detail page; manage them from a new /watchlist surface. Owned counts appear inline (placed + pending separately).
archivist_note: The first genuine new feature in a long string of UX and performance corrections. Watchlist also stands as foundation for future trade-adjacent surfaces — knowing what someone wants is the first signal that makes sharing collections useful.

added: Decks Owned tile on the dashboard — total deck count plus a Commander-format breakdown sub-line.
resolved: The Sets Collected tile (introduced the previous release) had its completion percentage moving based on other users' card imports. Replaced with a personal stat. The Sets page at /sets remains the authoritative completion view.
archivist_note: A quiet correction. The Sets tile was both inaccurate — cross-user signal pollution — and unhelpful, with no actionable next step. The Decks tile answers a question users actually ask.

added: Three dashboard tiles: Collection Value (total dollar worth of placed cards, finish-aware pricing), Sets Collected (later replaced by Decks Owned), and Recent Activity (latest eight collection events, linking to the audit log).
refined: Cross-page card counts now agree. The Drawers page switches from row-count to card-count — a drawer holding three rows of a 4-of now reads 12, not 3.
The Collection page headline shows placed cards only; pending cards surface as a separate sub-stat ('X cards / $Y pending placement →').
resolved: Three different 'how many cards do I have?' answers across the Collection, Drawers, and dashboard surfaces previously disagreed. They now answer the same question the same way.
archivist_note: A small change in the Drawers headline ('Total Cards' where it once said 'Total Rows') ripples across the whole understanding of 'how big is my collection?' — three places finally agree.

refined: The Decks page loads roughly 100× faster on a cold cache — from about 14 seconds to under 200 milliseconds. The combo and bracket panels are temporarily removed from deck surfaces while the analytics rebuild for both is in flight; trusted analytics (Health, mana curve, types, color pips, synergy, dead cards, tokens) all remain.
resolved: A long-standing performance problem on the Decks page (cold-cache visits making one external API call per deck on the request path) is closed.
archivist_note: An urgent fix slotted ahead of the planned next release. The combos and bracket subsystems aren't gone — they're dormant code waiting for the analytics rebuild that will reintroduce them with a faster path.

added: Left-sidebar layout replaces the previous horizontal navigation. Five grouped nav sections — Overview, Collection, Storage, Play, System — make destinations easier to scan.
Empty-state homepage for brand-new accounts: a clear first step and a numbered 'what comes next' path.
refined: Dashboard restructured around six Quick Action cards — the daily-workflow entry points surface as the page's primary content.
resolved: The Decks page's Edit/Delete column was being clipped at the right edge on standard desktop widths because the inherited page-wrapper width was sized for a no-sidebar layout. The anon-state topbar (login, register) was misaligned because the page-wrapper expected a sidebar that wasn't there.
archivist_note: The left-sidebar is the visible half; the harder half was rewriting every page's render paths to match the new shell without breaking the existing route map. We deferred the dashboard data tiles to a separate release so this one stayed about layout structure.

resolved: The End Game modal on the game tracker now renders with a solid background — the modal had been transparent over the dark overlay since the v3.10 era (an undefined CSS token reference, traced and fixed here).
The Drawers page total card count corrected (previously over-counted by ~500 cards because deck-type rows were leaking into the drawer count).
archivist_note: A quiet pass to set up the visual foundation that the navy aesthetic — and later the warm-charcoal Folio aesthetic — would build on. The one user-visible fix here (the End Game modal background) had been broken for longer than we realized.

refined: First step of the rebrand to Cartarch — the app now identifies as Cartarch in page titles, login and register copy, the welcome banner, and elsewhere across user-facing UI text.
archivist_note: This is the first half of the Cartarch rebrand: the text-only sweep. The wordmark, favicons, and icon set still showed 'Mana Archive' at this point — those would arrive a few releases later when the designer delivered. Splitting the rebrand across two releases let the design-token foundation and the navy reskin proceed in parallel without waiting on visual assets.
